mission.title: Install and Configure Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS)
mission.description: Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on AWS. It provides a consistent experience for managing Kubernetes clusters both on AWS and on-premises with EKS Anywhere. Key trade-offs include reliance on AWS infrastructure and potential costs associated with AWS services.

steps.title: Install the AWS CLI
steps.description:
The AWS CLI is required to interact with AWS services and manage your EKS cluster.

```bash

pip install awscli --upgrade --user

```

> Install the AWS CLI using pip.

steps.title: Create an EKS cluster
steps.description:
This command creates an EKS cluster with the specified Kubernetes version and node configuration.

```bash

aws eks create-cluster --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31 --role-arn arn:aws:iam::YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID:role/EKS-Cluster-Role --resources-vpc-config subnetIds=subnet-12345678,subnet-87654321

```

> Replace YOUR_ACCOUNT_ID with your actual AWS account ID.

steps.title: Update kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command updates your kubeconfig file to use the new EKS cluster.

```bash

aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster

```

> This allows kubectl to communicate with your EKS cluster.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your EKS cluster.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Ensure all nodes are in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your EKS cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/autoscaler/master/cluster-autoscaler/cloudprovider/aws/examples/cluster-autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kubernetes/metrics-server/master/deploy/1.8+/metrics-server-deployment.yaml

```

> This installs the cluster autoscaler.
> This installs the metrics server for monitoring.

uninstall.title: Delete the EKS cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the EKS cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

aws eks delete-cluster --name my-cluster

```

uninstall.title: Clean up orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any remaining resources such as load balancers and PVCs that may not be deleted automatically.

```bash

aws ec2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[*].LoadBalancerArn'

```

> List load balancers to manually delete.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the EKS cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your cluster, cordon nodes, upgrade the cluster, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, status: .status.conditions[] | select(.type=="Ready")}'

kubectl cordon <node-name>

aws eks update-cluster-version --name my-cluster --kubernetes-version 1.31

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup current node status.
> Replace <node-name> with the name of the node to cordon.
> Upgrade the EKS cluster to the new version.
> Verify that all nodes are in the 'Ready' state after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get nodes -o json | jq '.items[] | {name: .metadata.name, status: .status.conditions[] | select(.type=="Ready")}'

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your kubeconfig is not correctly configured or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Run 'aws eks update-kubeconfig --name my-cluster' to update your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'The cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The EKS cluster is still in the process of being created.

**Fix:**
Wait a few minutes and check the status with 'aws eks describe-cluster --name my-cluster'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient capacity in your region'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are not enough resources available in the specified region.

**Fix:**
Try creating the cluster in a different region or reduce the node count.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create cluster: AccessDeniedException'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the IAM role does not have sufficient permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the IAM role specified has the necessary permissions for EKS.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.31, improved IAM roles for service accounts.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.28, ensure that your workloads are compatible with 1.31.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with nodes listed as 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the AWS CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
